input_preview:
user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: NVIDIA and Google are leading 800 VDC adoption ahead of schedule, with small-volume shipments starting around Q3 2026; Vertiv and Delta are relevant suppliers.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["NVIDIA describes 800 VDC as next-generation power distribution for AI factories and lists electrical ecosystem partners including Delta and Vertiv.", "Vertiv says its 800 VDC power portfolio is planned for release in the second half of 2026 to support NVIDIA Rubin Ultra platforms.", "Taipei Times reports Delta plans small-volume 800V HVDC shipments to NVIDIA next quarter and says a major search-engine company has begun deploying +/-400V architecture."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/SUOHA_AI/status/2066442847893500161

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: The AI equity cycle moved from NVIDIA to memory/AMD, then to semiconductor equipment names such as LRCX, AMAT, and KLAC.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["NVIDIA reported record Q1 FY2027 revenue and data-center revenue, showing AI data-center demand as the main operating driver.", "AMD's IR materials describe Q1 2026 as driven by accelerating AI infrastructure demand and data-center growth.", "Micron reported record fiscal Q3 2026 results and highlighted data-center memory demand.", "Applied Materials reported Q2 FY2026 revenue growth and strong profitability.", "Lam Research reported March 2026 quarter results above the prior quarter on revenue and non-GAAP net income.", "KLA reported Q2 FY2026 revenue of $3.297B with most revenue from semiconductor process control."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/MMMusol/status/2070822154376273978

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Hyperscalers are spending heavily on AI data centers, NVIDIA captures the first revenue/profit wave, and memory becomes a next bottleneck.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["Alphabet expected 2026 capex in the $175B-$185B range for AI compute, Cloud demand, Services, and Other Bets.", "Meta raised 2026 capex guidance to $125B-$145B, citing data-center costs and AI infrastructure needs.", "NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 results show record data-center revenue, consistent with AI infrastructure spend flowing into NVIDIA systems.", "Micron prepared remarks say data-center revenue exceeded $25B and DRAM/NAND demand significantly exceeds supply."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: supports_core_claim_but_not_stock_sequence
source: https://x.com/MMMusol/status/2070778337589674257

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Long-term investors can map future AI/market opportunities into sector ETFs including smart grid, semiconductors, memory, and related infrastructure.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["First Trust states GRID tracks common stocks in the grid and electric energy infrastructure sector.", "Nasdaq describes QGRD as a benchmark for smart grid and electric infrastructure companies including meters, networks, storage, management, and software.", "Roundhill describes DRAM as a memory ETF with exposure to memory companies."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/BroBean88/status/2066201513131954344

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Optical-module earnings are certain while the semiconductor cycle has only just started.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["Sivers/GF evidence supports data-center optical interconnect relevance.", "KLA's fiscal 2026 results show solid semiconductor process-control revenue.", "KLA explains that advanced AI chips require precise process control across logic, memory, and packaging steps."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: insufficient_public_evidence
source: https://x.com/LynnChu26/status/2070689134054961490

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: TSMC moved glass substrate development from lab toward production validation with Ibiden and Innolux, extending CoWoS for AI chips.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["TSMC official CoWoS material supports CoWoS as an AI/HPC advanced-packaging technology.", "TSMC annual material discusses advanced packaging and 3D stacking, including CoWoS and photonic-engine roadmap work.", "Digitimes reports TSMC is working with Ibiden and Innolux on glass-substrate development, but the article is secondary and partly paywalled.", "Intel's glass-substrate release independently supports glass substrate as a serious advanced-packaging direction."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/TechFlowPost/status/2067202755434021162

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Many SPY/QQQ retail investors entered after 2020 and may underestimate drawdowns, making index DCA psychologically riskier than they expect.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["SEC investor resources warn about stock recommendations and investment decision-making based on social media.", "Vanguard frames trust and behavioral coaching as core to helping investors stay aligned with long-term goals and feelings.", "SEC's social-media investing alert warns investors to treat online investment information carefully and verify sources."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/caijingshujuku/status/2070871614435254671

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: NVDA is the obvious AI entry point, but broader AI opportunities emerge as the market discovers infrastructure bottlenecks beyond GPUs.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["NVIDIA's data-center revenue scale supports GPUs and AI systems as the visible center of AI infrastructure.", "Micron says DRAM/NAND demand significantly exceeds supply, supporting memory as an infrastructure bottleneck.", "Marvell says AI scaling depends on efficient data movement across accelerators, servers, racks, campuses, and data centers."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: supports_core_claim_but_not_opportunity_selection
source: https://x.com/F0HuazzZ/status/2066163767315173647

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Physical AI is a major future theme across robotics, autonomous systems, low-altitude economy, and AI hardware infrastructure.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["NVIDIA announced Cosmos world models, Isaac simulation frameworks, and Isaac GR00T models to accelerate robotics and physical AI.", "NVIDIA describes Isaac GR00T as a research initiative and development platform for robot foundation models and data pipelines."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/Bitcoin188/status/2069339852043325564

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Japanese stock bloggers can be useful supplemental information sources for U.S. tech, AI, and semiconductor investing after NISA growth.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["No official source in this pilot verifies the quality, identity, or track record of the recommended bloggers."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: source_discovery_only
source: https://x.com/daweifs/status/2066343124000190939

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: Claude's stock analysis is better than most retail investors because it removes emotional buy-high/sell-low bias through disciplined prompts.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["Vanguard emphasizes behavioral coaching, trust, and investor feelings/goals as important parts of advice quality.", "SEC investor resources warn against relying solely on social-media stock recommendations.", "NASAA warns AI-generated investment information may be inaccurate, incomplete, or misleading."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/crypto_oldk/status/2066450399100543368

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: 
claim: A seven-stock European photonics basket maps to CPO, InP lasers, silicon photonics, glass processing, and AI data-center optical interconnect themes; the stock basket itself is implied to be investable.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["Sivers says its laser arrays will be integrated into GlobalFoundries silicon-photonics reference designs for CPO, LPO, and other data-center interconnect architectures.", "Soitec positions engineered substrates for high-speed, robust data-center interconnects and network infrastructure connectivity.", "IQE describes InP as underpinning fibre-optic networks and as a platform for next-generation AI communications, cloud services, and 800G infrastructure.", "LPKF says its LIDE technology enables glass processing for advanced semiconductor packaging from ramp-up to high-volume manufacturing."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/SUOHA_AI/status/2066549392803295429

symbol:

symbol:

verdict: partial_support
